title: Locking / Lease SSOT

loop.observation: Locking detail manifests are inputs to the C9 generated lease reader, but coverage previously treated each detail page as independent loop debt.
loop.hypothesis: The locking root manifest can own the evidence loop for C9 lease semantics while detail pages delegate to that generated reader.
loop.execute: Generate the locking reader, load every declared detail manifest, verify source checks for file locks, leases, fencing, and metadata, and publish locking evidence.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails on stale generated docs, missing detail pages, missing source anchors, or malformed locking fragments.
loop.retrospective: C9 locking knowledge remains executable and generated while fragment files stay focused on one lease concept each.

summary: C9 Locking / Lease owns local file lock primitives, task DB sidecar lease registry mechanics, and fencing token infrastructure rules.
C9 does not decide runtime eligibility, task lifecycle state semantics, or provider process execution.
responsibilities: task DB sidecar lease registry persistence
single-writer critical sections for local JSON task DB mutation
non_responsibilities: runtime scheduling eligibility decisions owned by C5
task state transition contract owned by public riido-contracts C1
provider process execution owned by C4 Provider Runtime
invariants: C9 provides primitives only; lock acquisition, release, sidecar update, and fencing token mechanics do not select eligible tasks.
Local JSON task DB mutation happens only under the shared task DB .lock file.
Fencing token is a task-scoped monotonic counter.
Lease pin equals the C5 RuntimeLease pair of RuntimeID and CapabilityFingerprint.
File lock is a local-only primitive; remote/distributed claims require an adapter with the same C9 meaning.

source_checks.name: file-lock-with
source_checks.contains: func WithFile(ctx context.Context, path string, fn func() error)

assertions: locking reader docs are generated from this manifest and fragments
source checks prove the local file lock, lease registry, fencing, and metadata anchors still exist
C9 locking docs must not claim C5 scheduling or C4 provider execution ownership
